title: Reverse Engineering
description: Static and dynamic analysis techniques for understanding binaries.

topics.title: Static Analysis
topics.explanation:
Static analysis examines code/data without running the program:
- Disassembly & decompilation
- String extraction
- Import/export analysis
- Cross-reference (xref) tracking
- Control flow graph construction

Start static when malware concerns exist or anti-debug is suspected.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Static Analysis
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Static Analysis in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Dynamic Analysis
topics.explanation:
Dynamic analysis runs the program under observation:
- Breakpoints (software/hardware)
- Memory watchpoints
- API hooking/logging
- Trace recording

Combines with static findings to confirm hypotheses.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Dynamic Analysis
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Dynamic Analysis in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Disassemblers
topics.explanation:
Disassemblers convert machine code to assembly:
- **Ghidra** — free, decompiler, scripting
- **IDA Free** — industry standard (limited)
- **Binary Ninja** — modern UI, HLIL

Skills: rename functions, define structures, follow xrefs, patch bytes (for research VMs only).
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Disassemblers
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Disassemblers in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Decompilers
topics.explanation:
Decompilers reconstruct high-level code from assembly. Accuracy varies with:
- Correct function boundaries
- Type information
- Calling convention
- Obfuscation

Always verify decompiler output against disassembly.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Decompilers
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Decompilers in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Function Analysis
topics.explanation:
Workflow:
1. Find entry via xref/string/API
2. Determine signature (args, return)
3. Rename and comment
4. Map local variables
5. Identify key branches/loops

Look for constants (magic numbers), string refs, and API calls as hints.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Function Analysis
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Function Analysis in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Control Flow Analysis
topics.explanation:
Control Flow Graphs show basic blocks and edges. Recognize:
- if/else → conditional jumps
- while/for → backward jumps
- switch → jump tables
- Short-circuit evaluation patterns

Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Import Tables
topics.explanation:
The import directory lists external dependencies. Delay-load imports resolve at first call. Analyze imports to understand:
- Graphics APIs (d3d11.dll, opengl32.dll)
- Network (ws2_32.dll)
- Anti-debug (IsDebuggerPresent)
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Import Tables

Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Import Tables in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Export Tables
topics.explanation:
Exports name publicly available functions. Games may export mod SDK functions. Analyze exports in engine DLLs to find:
- Factory functions
- Interface getters
- Plugin entry points

Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: String Analysis
topics.description: Finding and using strings in binaries.
topics.explanation:
Strings reveal:
- Error messages → code paths
- Config keys → settings system
- Class/func names (debug builds)
- URLs and file paths

Tools: Ghidra strings window, FLOSS, strings.exe. Follow xrefs from strings to functions.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for String Analysis

Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining String Analysis in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Signature Scanning
topics.description: Pattern matching in code and memory.
topics.explanation:
Signatures (AOB — array of bytes) identify code patterns despite ASLR:
- Wildcards for relocations: `48 8B ?? ?? ?? ?? ??`
- Unique prologue bytes
- IDA FLIRT signatures for library identification

Used in research tools and mod frameworks for locating functions.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Signature Scanning
